title: Bounded XML Decoding, Encodings, Entities, and Structural Security · Runtime Contract: Feed Go Charset Contract
summary: The service unmarshals an unbounded body, disables strict decoding to accept broken feeds, expands custom entities, ignores depth, and silently replaces unsupported encodings. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go charset contract increment in strict bounded XML admission boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Use an allowlisted CharsetReader, reconcile transport and XML declarations, and reject unsupported or conflicting encodings.
Keep encoding/xml strict mode, inspect tokens, reject malformed nesting and trailing documents, and never install arbitrary entity maps.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Charset Contract · Theory Feed Go Charset Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 4808 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit bounded strict XML while rejecting malformed, deep, entity, encoding, and trailing cases; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use an allowlisted CharsetReader, reconcile transport and XML declarations, and reject unsupported or conflicting encodings.
